reviews.strongestCounterargument: The queue-free 100 ms slot simulator can manufacture overload and does not represent provider-side queues, variable service time, regional recovery, or protocol-aware Retry-After behavior, so the numeric operating point may not transfer.
reviews.unsupportedClaimRisk: The weakest claim was that an eight-retry-per-second token bucket was a suitable operating recommendation; only one nonzero refill rate and one burst capacity were measured, so the article now treats it solely as a mechanism treatment.
reviews.reproductionBarrier: The dependency-free artifacts are easy to rerun, but production confirmation requires provider status semantics, the real layered retry stack, anonymized arrival and incident traces, service-time distributions, and a safe failure-injection proxy.
revisions: Corrected the independent-fault control from 10% to the Markov process's 3.5714% stationary mean and regenerated all 4,800 repeat-policy rows, aggregates, intervals, and the figure.

Added the matched-mean correlation result showing 263.2 versus 174.0 peak attempts per second and 2.44 points lower completion, making temporal dependence rather than mean error rate the stronger mechanism claim.
Narrowed the retry-token treatment from a deployable setting to one tested point and required a preregistered refill-rate and burst-capacity sweep before production selection.
Preserved the negative result that full jitter lowered retry latency but increased the peak relative to deterministic backoff in the declared fault process.

reviews.strongestCounterargument: Without running the checkpoints on target GPUs and a private corpus, the article cannot establish whether 8B quality, 1B BF16 compatibility, or NVFP4 throughput wins for any specific deployment.
reviews.unsupportedClaimRisk: The weakest public signal is NVIDIA's up-to-two-times NVFP4 throughput statement because the release summary does not expose a common hardware, batch, sequence, concurrency, runtime, and latency table; the article now treats it only as a canary hypothesis.
reviews.reproductionBarrier: A credible adoption test needs supported NVIDIA hardware, pinned vLLM 0.25.0, all three large checkpoints, a representative judged query set, exact and ANN indexes, and enough temporary storage for dual-index migration.
revisions: Separated the 32,768-token checkpoint capability from the 4,096-token NIM and evaluation boundary, preventing model-card context from being presented as packaged-service validation.
Added a four-cell old/new query-encoder by old/new document-index compatibility audit so the shared-space statement cannot be mistaken for safe in-place migration or rollback.
Added locally derived float-vector storage arithmetic and explicitly excluded ANN overhead and unmeasured self-hosting prices from the comparison.
Added fail-closed runtime conformance and rollback-rehearsal requirements before retrieval scoring.

reviews.strongestCounterargument: The public incident account is provider-authored and still under assessment, so it cannot prove that any proposed topology or isolation control would have prevented the specific compromise.
reviews.unsupportedClaimRisk: The weakest inference was that a separate account or cluster would have prevented lateral movement; the article now states that this counterfactual is unproven and conditions the benefit on peering, identity federation, metadata, and control-plane enforcement.
reviews.reproductionBarrier: Adoption requires an inventory of executable ingestion paths, an isolated target account, production-equivalent workload identity, synthetic canary credentials, sandbox and network-policy support, and authorized escape testing.
revisions: Separated incident facts from locally derived control mappings and added explicit denominator and ongoing-assessment limits instead of treating the disclosure as a measured architecture study.
Added a hostile-evidence matrix covering metadata access, path traversal, credential scope, mutable revisions, signature tampering, and private control-plane access with release-blocking outcomes.
Narrowed the cluster-separation recommendation to a blast-radius control whose effectiveness must be tested rather than an asserted incident counterfactual.
Added a four-tier execution matrix so fixed-format research workflows retain a fast path without silently admitting executable loaders.
